reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with a high degree of structural adherence to the provided script. This mispronunciation affects the specific meaning of the noun phrase but preserves the overall sentence structure and core message regarding persistence. Regarding the target paralinguistic feature 'Stress', the model appears to deliver the sentence with the appropriate flow, and we infer that the emphasis on 'every day' (00:02-00:03) is applied as requested within the correct prosodic contour. reasoning_model_2: The model fails to strictly follow the negative constraint of the command ('Please read this sentence...'). Instead of reading only the target text, it hallucinates a repetitive preamble: 'Every day, every day for skin care?' (00:00-00:03) before reading the actual sentence. model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. Although Model 1 has a specific phonetic error (substituting 'care' with 'tear'), it strictly adheres to the requested sentence structure and format. Model 2, while getting the word 'care' right in its second half, introduces significant hallucinations by adding a repetitive opening phrase. This violation of the 'read this sentence' constraint is a more severe failure of instruction following than Model 1's local pronunciation error. Model 1 demonstrates better control over the generation task.
